title: ContractDraft
description: Output schema for the contract-drafter agent. Represents a fully drafted contract plus structured metadata for review and approval.

contract_type.description: Contract type enum: owner_gc | subcontract | change_order | purchase_order | letter_of_intent | nda | msa | equipment_lease | insurance_certificate | lien_waiver | other | unknown

template_id.description: The template_id used, if mode=template. Null for negotiated mode.

parties.description: Parties to the contract — echoed from input.

effective_date.description: ISO-8601 effective date — echoed from input.

expiration_date.description: ISO-8601 expiration date — echoed from input or null.

total_value_usd.description: Contract value in USD — echoed from input or null.

title.description: Display title for the drafted contract (e.g., 'Subcontract Agreement — ABC Framing LLC — Project Alpha').

summary.description: 100–200 word plain-English summary of what this contract does, who the parties are, what's covered, and the key financial/time terms.

body_markdown.description: The full drafted contract as Markdown. This is the actual contract document. Must include all articles/sections, definitions, and a signature block. Must end with the AI-generated draft disclaimer footer.

sections.description: Section table of contents — one entry per major article or section in body_markdown.

heading.description: Section heading as it appears in the document.

summary.description: 1–2 sentence summary of what this section covers.

variables_used.description: For template mode: map of variable name → value used. For negotiated mode: map of key decision points → value chosen.

key_terms_addressed.description: For each item in key_terms_requested, how it is reflected in the draft. Key is the topic; value is a plain-English explanation.

assumptions_made.description: Every place the agent filled in a missing variable or made a judgment call. Required for transparency.

topic.description: Which provision or variable was assumed.

assumption.description: What value or clause was chosen.

why_made.description: Why this assumption was reasonable given the inputs.

attorney_review_focus.description: At least 3 items — specific provisions counsel should scrutinize, with a concrete question to ask.

topic.description: Name of the provision or issue.

why.description: Why counsel should review this specifically.

suggested_question.description: A specific question for counsel to answer about this provision.

disclaimer.description: Must be exactly: 'AI-generated analysis. This is not legal advice. Review with qualified counsel before relying on it for any binding decision.'

citations.description: Legal citations, if any. Typically empty for drafted contracts — do not cite cases or statutes without verified sources.
